description: B-022: Chi-Srv-01 Companion Service - FastAPI server with MariaDB, Ollama AI, delta sync, and backup receiving

userStories.title: Project Scaffolding and Configuration
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the OBD2-Server project scaffolded with FastAPI, configuration, and dependency management so all subsequent stories have a foundation to build on.
acceptanceCriteria: Create README.md with project description, setup instructions placeholder
Create .gitignore for Python projects (venv, __pycache__, .env, *.pyc, .pytest_cache)
Create pyproject.toml with project metadata, Python 3.11+ requirement
Create requirements.txt with: fastapi, uvicorn, sqlalchemy, aiomysql, pydantic-settings, python-multipart, httpx, alembic, pytest, pytest-asyncio
Create src/__init__.py (empty)
Create src/main.py with FastAPI app skeleton and lifespan handler (startup: log config; shutdown: placeholder)

Create src/config.py with Pydantic Settings reading from .env: DATABASE_URL (required), OLLAMA_BASE_URL (default http://localhost:11434), OLLAMA_MODEL (default llama3.1:8b), API_KEY (required), BACKUP_DIR (default ./data/backups), PORT (default 8000), LOG_LEVEL (default INFO), MAX_BACKUP_SIZE_MB (default 100), BACKUP_RETENTION_COUNT (default 30)
Create .env.example with all config variables and descriptions
Add GET /api/v1/health endpoint returning {"status": "healthy", "version": "1.0.0"}
App starts with: uvicorn src.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000
Create tests/__init__.py and tests/conftest.py with basic fixtures
Create tests/test_health.py with test: health endpoint returns 200 with status and version
Create tests/test_config.py with tests: config loads variables, raises on missing DATABASE_URL, raises on missing API_KEY, uses defaults for optional vars
All tests pass with pytest tests/

userStories.title: API Key Authentication Middleware
userStories.description: As a developer, I need API key authentication on all endpoints (except /health) so only authorized devices can push data.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/auth.py implementing FastAPI dependency that extracts X-API-Key header
API key compared against API_KEY from config using hmac.compare_digest() for constant-time comparison
Missing key returns 401 with {"detail": "Missing API key"}
Invalid key returns 401 with {"detail": "Invalid API key"}
/api/v1/health remains exempt from auth (no dependency)
Create placeholder POST /api/v1/sync endpoint that requires auth and returns 200 {"status": "placeholder"}
Create placeholder POST /api/v1/analyze endpoint that requires auth and returns 200 {"status": "placeholder"}
Create placeholder POST /api/v1/backup endpoint that requires auth and returns 200 {"status": "placeholder"}

Create tests/test_auth.py with tests: POST /sync without X-API-Key returns 401 Missing, POST /sync with wrong key returns 401 Invalid, POST /sync with correct key returns non-401, GET /health without key returns 200, POST /analyze without key returns 401, POST /backup without key returns 401
All tests pass

userStories.title: MariaDB Database Schema and Connection
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the MariaDB database schema mirroring the Pi's SQLite tables plus server-only columns and tables.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/database/__init__.py, src/database/connection.py with async SQLAlchemy 2.x engine and session factory using aiomysql
Create src/models/__init__.py that exports all models
Create SQLAlchemy models for 8 synced tables: realtime_data, statistics, profiles, vehicle_info, ai_recommendations, connection_log, alert_log, calibration_sessions
Each synced table has: id (autoincrement PK), source_id (INT NOT NULL), source_device (VARCHAR(64) NOT NULL), synced_at (DATETIME NOT NULL), sync_batch_id (VARCHAR(64) nullable)
Each synced table has UNIQUE(source_device, source_id) constraint
realtime_data columns: timestamp, parameter_name, value, unit, profile_id (FK to profiles.id)
statistics columns: profile_id (FK), parameter_name, min_value, max_value, avg_value, count, std_dev, recorded_at
profiles columns: name, description, created_at, is_active
vehicle_info columns: vin, make, model, year, engine, fuel_type, updated_at
ai_recommendations columns: profile_id (FK), category, recommendation, confidence, model_used, created_at
connection_log columns: event_type, timestamp, adapter_name, protocol, details
alert_log columns: profile_id (FK), parameter_name, alert_type, threshold_value, actual_value, timestamp
calibration_sessions columns: profile_id (FK), session_type, started_at, completed_at, status, notes
Create server-only table: sync_history (batch_id VARCHAR PK, device_id, started_at, completed_at, tables_synced JSON, rows_inserted INT, rows_updated INT, status ENUM)
Create server-only table: analysis_history (analysis_id VARCHAR PK, profile_source_id, drive_start, drive_end, model, processing_time_ms, status, created_at)
Create server-only table: devices (device_id VARCHAR PK, display_name, last_seen, api_key_hash, created_at)
Create scripts/init_db.py that creates all tables idempotently using metadata.create_all()
Create deploy/setup-mariadb.sh that creates obd2db and obd2db_test databases, grants to user obd2
Configure Alembic with alembic.ini and alembic/env.py pointing to models
Create initial Alembic migration matching schema
Update src/main.py lifespan to verify MariaDB connection on startup

Create tests/test_database.py with tests: init_db creates all 11 tables (query SHOW TABLES), realtime_data has correct columns (DESCRIBE), UNIQUE constraint prevents duplicate (source_device, source_id), FK constraint enforced on profile_id, sync_history accepts insert, init_db is idempotent
All tests pass against obd2db_test database

userStories.title: Delta Sync Endpoint
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the POST /sync endpoint that accepts delta data from EclipseTuner and upserts into MariaDB.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/routers/sync.py with POST /api/v1/sync endpoint
Create Pydantic request model: SyncRequest with deviceId (str required), batchId (str required), tables (dict of table_name to TableData)
TableData model: lastSyncedId (int), rows (list of dicts)
Validate table names against allowed list: realtime_data, statistics, profiles, vehicle_info, ai_recommendations, connection_log, alert_log, calibration_sessions. Return 422 for unknown tables.
Create src/services/sync_service.py with sync logic
For each table: upsert rows using INSERT ... ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE with key (source_device, source_id)
Map incoming row 'id' field to source_id column. MariaDB generates its own id.
Server sets synced_at=NOW(), source_device=deviceId, sync_batch_id=batchId on each row
All upserts run in single transaction. On error: rollback entire batch.
Create sync_history record with batch_id, device_id, started_at, completed_at, tables_synced, rows_inserted, rows_updated, status
Return response: {status, batchId, tablesProcessed: {table: {inserted, updated, errors}}, syncedAt, driveDataReceived}
driveDataReceived is true if connection_log rows contain event_type='drive_end'
Add MAX_SYNC_PAYLOAD_MB to config (default 10). Return 413 if payload exceeds limit.
On MariaDB error: return 500 with {detail: 'Sync failed', batchId}, set sync_history status='failed'

Create tests/test_sync.py with tests: sync 5 rows returns 200 and DB has 5 rows, sync empty tables returns 200, sync fake_table returns 422, sync same batch twice is idempotent, missing deviceId returns 422, missing batchId returns 422, connection_log with drive_end sets driveDataReceived true, connection_log without drive_end sets driveDataReceived false, sync_history record created, constraint violation rolls back entire batch
All tests pass

userStories.title: AI Analysis Endpoint
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the POST /analyze endpoint that triggers Ollama AI analysis on drive data stored in MariaDB.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/routers/analyze.py with POST /api/v1/analyze endpoint
Create Pydantic request model: AnalyzeRequest with profileId (int), driveStartTime (ISO8601 str), driveEndTime (ISO8601 str), parameters (list of str), focusAreas (list of str)
Create src/services/analysis_service.py with analysis logic
Create src/services/ollama_client.py with httpx.AsyncClient for Ollama API calls
Query MariaDB for realtime_data rows within time window matching parameters
If zero rows: return 200 with {status: 'ok', recommendations: [], message: 'No drive data found for the specified time window'}
Create src/services/prompts/drive_analysis.py with system and user prompt templates
System prompt describes 1998 Eclipse GST tuning context. User prompt contains parameter statistics (min, max, avg, count) and focus areas.
Send to Ollama POST {OLLAMA_BASE_URL}/api/chat with {model, messages, stream: false}
Add ANALYSIS_TIMEOUT_SECONDS to config (default 120). Return 504 if exceeded.
Parse Ollama response message.content into recommendations with rank, category, recommendation, confidence
Store recommendations in ai_recommendations table
Create analysis_history record with analysis_id, profile_source_id, drive_start, drive_end, model, processing_time_ms, status
Return: {status, analysisId, recommendations, model, processingTimeMs}
If Ollama unreachable: return 503 with {detail: 'Ollama unavailable', ollamaUrl, model}
If Ollama returns error: return 502 with {detail: 'Ollama error', ollamaError}

Create tests/test_analyze.py with tests: seed data and mock Ollama returns recommendations (verify DB), no data returns empty recommendations, Ollama connection error returns 503, Ollama HTTP 500 returns 502, timeout returns 504 with analysis_history status='failed'
All tests pass

userStories.title: Auto-Analysis on Drive Data Receipt
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the sync endpoint to automatically trigger AI analysis when it detects that a completed drive's data was synced.
acceptanceCriteria: Modify sync_service.py: after successful sync with driveDataReceived=true, enqueue async analysis task via asyncio.create_task()
Auto-analysis extracts drive boundaries from synced connection_log rows (most recent drive_start/drive_end pair)
If connection_log not in sync batch but driveDataReceived inferred: skip auto-analysis, log WARNING 'Cannot determine drive boundaries without connection_log'
Async task calls same analysis logic as /analyze endpoint (shared service function)
Sync response includes autoAnalysisTriggered: true when task enqueued
If Ollama unavailable: log WARNING 'Auto-analysis skipped: Ollama unavailable', sync response has autoAnalysisTriggered: false
If auto-analysis fails: log ERROR, no effect on sync success (sync still returns 200)

Create tests/test_auto_analysis.py with tests: sync with drive_end triggers auto-analysis (verify analysis_history after async wait), sync without connection_log has autoAnalysisTriggered false, sync with only drive_start has driveDataReceived false, Ollama unavailable still returns sync 200 with autoAnalysisTriggered false, Ollama exception during analysis creates analysis_history with status='failed'
All tests pass

userStories.title: Backup Receiver Endpoint
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the POST /backup endpoint that accepts file uploads from EclipseTuner and stores them with timestamps.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/routers/backup.py with POST /api/v1/backup endpoint accepting multipart/form-data
Request fields: file (UploadFile), type (str: database|logs|config), deviceId (str)
Create src/services/backup_service.py with file storage logic
Validate type is one of allowed values, else 422
Validate file extension is .db, .log, .json, or .gz, else 422 with detail 'File extension not allowed. Allowed: .db, .log, .json, .gz'
Validate deviceId is non-empty, else 422
Check file size against MAX_BACKUP_SIZE_MB. Return 413 with {detail: 'File too large. Max: 100MB'} if exceeded.
Store file at {BACKUP_DIR}/{deviceId}/{type}/{stem}-{YYYY-MM-DDTHH-MM-SS}.{ext}
Create directory structure with os.makedirs(..., exist_ok=True)
Return: {status: 'ok', filename, storedAt, sizeBytes}
After storing: count files in directory. If count > BACKUP_RETENTION_COUNT, delete oldest files until count = limit. Never delete file just uploaded.
Rotation safety: never delete last remaining file (minimum 1 kept)

Create tests/test_backup.py (use tmp_path fixture for BACKUP_DIR) with tests: upload .db file stores correctly, upload .exe returns 422, invalid type returns 422, empty deviceId returns 422, file too large returns 413, upload 32 files with retention=30 keeps 30, non-existent directory created, single file not deleted by rotation
All tests pass

userStories.title: Health Endpoint with Component Status
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the GET /health endpoint to return detailed status of all service components.
acceptanceCriteria: Modify src/routers/health.py to return full component status
Response: {status, version, components: {api, mysql, ollama, ollamaModel}, lastSync, uptime}
components.mysql: 'up' if SELECT 1 succeeds within 3 seconds, 'down' otherwise
components.ollama: 'up' if GET {OLLAMA_BASE_URL}/ returns 200 within 5 seconds, 'down' otherwise
components.ollamaModel: model name if GET /api/tags lists configured model, 'not_loaded' otherwise, 'unknown' if Ollama down
status: 'healthy' if mysql=up AND ollama=up AND model loaded; 'degraded' if mysql=up but ollama issues; 'unhealthy' if mysql=down
lastSync: query sync_history for most recent success, return {deviceId, syncedAt, batchId} or null if none
uptime: calculate from app startup time (stored in lifespan), format as 'Xd Yh Zm'
No authentication required
Must respond within 5 seconds (component check timeouts ensure this)

Update tests/test_health.py with tests: all healthy returns status healthy, Ollama down returns degraded, MariaDB down returns unhealthy, no sync_history returns lastSync null, with sync_history returns correct lastSync, uptime matches pattern
All tests pass

userStories.title: systemd Service and Deployment
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the companion service to run as a systemd unit on Chi-Srv-01 with auto-start and restart-on-failure.
acceptanceCriteria: Create deploy/OBD2-Server.service systemd unit: Type=simple, ExecStart with uvicorn --host 0.0.0.0 --port $PORT, Restart=on-failure, RestartSec=10, After=network.target mariadb.service, EnvironmentFile, WorkingDirectory
Create deploy/install-service.sh: copies service file to /etc/systemd/system/, daemon-reload, enable, start, status check
Ensure deploy/setup-mariadb.sh exists and is executable (creates obd2db, obd2db_test, grants to obd2)

Create docs/setup-guide.md with sections: Prerequisites (Python 3.11+, MariaDB 10.x, Ollama), OS packages, venv creation, pip install, MariaDB setup, Ollama install and model pull, .env configuration, service installation, firewall rules (port 8000), verification (curl /health), troubleshooting

Create tests/test_deployment.py with tests: OBD2-Server.service file exists and contains ExecStart/Restart/After, install-service.sh exists and is executable, setup-mariadb.sh exists and is executable, setup-guide.md exists and contains required sections
All tests pass
Manual verification: CIO confirms service starts and restarts on failure
